page.content:
The data presented in Table 1 offers a comprehensive overview of the genetic variations associated with the TP-023 locus, specifically focusing on the presence of two copies of the relevant alleles. The metrics outlined, including NIPA1BP1-BP2 and SNRPNBP2-BP3, provide insight into the chromosomal configurations and their implications for genetic expression. Notably, all subjects analyzed exhibit a "Not affected" status, suggesting that the presence of these two copies does not correlate with adverse phenotypic outcomes in this context. 

Furthermore, the TRPM1BP4-BP5 measure reinforces this observation by indicating a consistent pattern across the sampled individuals, all of whom show similar genotypic profiles. This consistency in genetic makeup highlights the importance of understanding the role of these specific gene copies in maintaining normal physiological function. The data suggests that the two copies of the alleles in question may play a protective role, mitigating potential deleterious effects commonly associated with variations in this genetic region. Such findings are pivotal for advancing our understanding of genetic resilience and may inform future research avenues aimed at elucidating the mechanisms underlying genetic influences on health.
